design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore handcrafted environments → discover mechanical/symbolic puzzles → manipulate contraptions and decipher codes → unlock new areas → progress toward freeing Catherine from Gehn's captivity
design_philosophy.primary_tension: World-state deterioration (Age of Riven slowly collapsing) vs. player's ability to solve interconnected puzzles; moral tension between confronting Gehn and understanding his destructive influence on the world
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a detective-engineer exploring an alien world: reading environmental clues, understanding Victorian-industrial machinery, decoding symbolic language systems, and unraveling a family conspiracy across dimensional boundaries
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Riven rejects narrative convenience in favor of environmental storytelling and mechanical logic. Puzzles emerge organically from understanding fictional culture and physics rather than arbitrary inventory combinations. The game trusts players to infer goals vaguely rather than explicitly instructing them. Larger, more complex world with five interconnected islands replaces Myst's hub-and-spoke Age-travel structure, demanding genuine spatial navigation rather than teleportation

difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save system with exploration-based progress (Zip Mode available to skip to already-explored areas)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through optional Zip Mode navigation. Players can miss clues by skipping areas; no difficulty selector mentioned. Puzzle complexity cannot be adjusted. No fail states—only progression gates

progression.unlock_mechanism: Puzzle solutions unlock mechanical access to new islands and areas. Five islands of Age of Riven serve as progression gates. Players can explore without fulfilling objectives but must solve puzzles to access certain regions
replayability_hooks: Environmental clues embedded in surroundings reward careful observation
Multiple puzzle solutions depending on player interpretation
Symbolic/mechanical systems require understanding fictional culture—incentivizes second playthroughs for deeper comprehension
Optional Zip Mode changes first-playthrough pacing and clue discovery

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: volcanic landscape with steep cliffs, crater lakes bestrewn with Victorian-style mechanical artifacts (elevators, pipes, levers, roller coasters)
Mechanical symbolism: player must understand purpose and physical principles of artifacts and their role in fictional culture to progress
Pickup items carry narrative weight: examined items reveal clues to puzzles or world-setting information
Implicit plot continuation: events follow directly from Myst and companion novel The Book of Atrus
Spatial narrative: five interconnected islands of Age of Riven embody Gehn's destructive rule through world deterioration

faction_indicators: Gehn's autocratic rule and self-declared rulership of Riven
Atrus as benevolent counterbalance: rescuer figure seeking to free Catherine
Sirrus and Achenar (antagonists from Myst) manipulate Catherine by tricking her into returning to Riven
danger_signals: Age of Riven physically collapsing due to Gehn's destructive rule
Star Fissure: rift leading into mysterious void—existential threat to Age stability
Thirty-year imprisonment of Gehn with severed exit routes: desperation of isolated power figure
Catherine held hostage: captivity as tool of control and manipulation
environmental_storytelling: Victorian-industrial aesthetics reflect Gehn's worldview: mechanical dominance over nature
Volcanic landscape with crater lakes: geological instability mirrors social/political collapse
Mechanical contraptions throughout world: evidence of Gehn's engineering ambitions and resource extraction
Five distinct islands: fragmented world structure suggesting advanced civilization in decline

sources_used: wikipedia: Riven - Wikipedia (Gameplay section, Plot section, Development overview)
